steps.title: Rearrange and factor the equation
steps.content:
Starting with $x - a = (x - a)(x - 24)$, move the right side to the left side:
$$x - a - (x - a)(x - 24) = 0$$
Factor out the common factor $(x - a)$:
$$(x - a)[1 - (x - 24)] = 0$$
Simplify the bracket:
$$(x - a)(25 - x) = 0$$
This factored form tells us that $x = a$ or $x = 25$ are solutions.

steps.title: Test all three options
steps.content:
Option I: $x = a$
This makes the first factor $(x - a) = 0$ in our factored equation. ✓

Option II: $x = 24$
Substitute into the original equation: $24 - a = (24 - a)(0) = 0$.
This requires $a = 24$, but we're given $a > 25$. ✗

Option III: $x = 25$
This makes the second factor $(25 - x) = 0$ in our factored equation. ✓

Only options I and III are valid. That matches choice B — B is correct.
